items.headline: U.S. Markets Closed for Weekend; Focus Shifts to Next Week's Open
items.summary: As of Saturday, April 12, 2026, U.S. stock markets are closed. Trading activity for the week concluded on Friday, April 11, 2026. The past week saw a sharp rebound in markets, largely driven by optimism surrounding a temporary ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran, which helped ease oil prices and stagflation concerns. However, overall market sentiment remains mixed, with consumer confidence plunging due to geopolitical tensions and high inflation. Investors will be evaluating weekend news for impacts on Monday's opening.

items.statContext: Down 0.1% for the day, up 3.6% for the week. Down 0.4% year-to-date.
Up 0.4% for the day, up 4.7% for the week. Down 1.5% year-to-date.
Down 0.6% for the day, up 3% for the week. Down 0.3% year-to-date.

items.headline: Nvidia Leads Tech Gains
items.summary: Technology stocks provided a boost on Friday, with Nvidia showing strength amidst broader market declines for many S&P 500 companies.

items.headline: Broadcom Surges on Strong Performance
items.summary: Broadcom continued its strong performance from last week, contributing to the positive movement in the technology sector.

items.headline: Eli Lilly Falls
items.summary: Health care stocks were among those that lost ground, contributing to the broader decline in many S&P 500 constituents.

items.headline: Charles Schwab Declines
items.summary: Financial company stocks experienced declines on Friday, with Charles Schwab closing lower.

items.headline: Ceasefire Optimism Drives Market Rebound Amid Fragile Peace Talks
items.summary: A temporary ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran led to a surge in stock markets and a drop in oil prices, easing stagflation fears. High-level talks are underway, but the agreement's durability remains a market concern.

items.headline: Inflation Concerns Persist as Consumer Prices Rise
items.summary: March saw consumer prices rise 3.3% annually, the highest in over two years, with new car prices up 12.6%. Core CPI increased 2.6% year-over-year, while the Fed's preferred PCE gauge showed a 2.8% increase. Year-ahead inflation expectations surged to 4.8% in April.

items.headline: Q1 Earnings Season Kicks Off Next Week with Major Banks and Tech
items.summary: The first-quarter earnings season begins next week, with major banks including Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, Citigroup, Bank of America, and Wells Fargo, as well as tech giant Netflix, scheduled to report. Over half of S&P 500 companies have issued positive EPS guidance for Q1.

items.headline: Tech Sector Faces Rotation Amid Strong AI Demand and Valuation Compression
items.summary: Despite strong underlying demand for AI and positive earnings from key players like Microsoft, the tech-heavy NASDAQ has seen a downturn, with investors rotating out of the sector. Tech valuations have compressed back to pre-AI boom levels.

items.headline: Consumer Sentiment Plunges to Multi-Decade Lows in April
items.summary: U.S. consumer sentiment plummeted by 11% in April, reaching multi-decade lows. Consumers expressed increased concerns over high prices, weaker asset values, and the ongoing Iran conflict.
